Christianity teaches people to be joyful during difficult times: these experiences are sent from God’s love; and trials in doing what is right will strengthen our qualities now, and our reward later. Let us make sure, during times of trial, that patience, not passion, guides us: whatever is said or done, let patience control it. When the work of patience is complete, it will provide everything necessary for our Christian journey and struggle. We should not pray so much for relief from suffering, as for wisdom to use it correctly. And who does not want wisdom to guide them during trials, both in controlling their own feelings, and in managing their affairs? Here is something that answers every discouraging thought when we go to God feeling weak and foolish. If, after all, someone says, This may be the case with some, but I fear I will not succeed, the promise is, To anyone who asks, it will be given. A person whose focus is entirely on their spiritual and eternal well-being, and who remains committed to God’s purposes, will become wise through suffering, will remain devoted, and rise above trials and opposition. When our faith and spirits rise and fall with external circumstances, there will be inconsistency in our words and actions. This may not always expose people to contempt in the world, but such ways cannot please God. No situation in life prevents rejoicing in God. People of low status can rejoice if they are elevated to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom of God; and the wealthy can rejoice in humbling experiences that lead to a humble attitude. Wealth is destructive. Then, let those who are wealthy rejoice in God’s grace, which makes and keeps them humble; and in the trials and challenges that teach them to find happiness in and from God, not from temporary pleasures.

Not everyone who suffers is blessed; but he who with patience and perseverance goes through all difficulties in doing what is right. Suffering cannot make us miserable, unless it is our own fault. The tested Christian will be crowned. The crown of life is promised to all who have God’s love ruling in their hearts. Every soul that truly loves God will have its trials in this world fully rewarded in the world above, where love is perfected. God’s commands and his guidance test people's hearts, and reveal the attitudes that dominate them. But nothing sinful in the heart or actions can be attributed to God. He is not the source of badness, even though his testing exposes it. Those who blame sin on their nature, or on their circumstances, or claim they cannot avoid sinning, wrong God as if he were the cause of sin. Suffering, as sent by God, is intended to draw out our good qualities, but not our flaws. The origin of evil and temptation is in our own hearts. Stop the beginnings of sin, or all the resulting problems will be entirely our fault. God does not want people to die, just as he has no hand in their sin; but both sin and misery are due to themselves. As the sun remains the same in nature and influence, even though the earth and clouds often get in the way, making it seem different, so God is unchanging, and our changes and shadows do not come from any changes or alterations in him. What the sun is in nature, God is in grace, guidance, and glory; and infinitely more. Just as every good gift comes from God, so does being born again, along with all its holy and happy consequences. A true Christian becomes a different person than they were before experiencing Divine grace, as if they were formed anew. We should devote all our abilities to God’s service, so that we may be a kind of first offering of his creation.

Instead of blaming God during trials, let us open our ears and hearts to learn what he teaches through them. And if people would control their words, they must control their passions. The worst thing we can bring to any disagreement is anger. Here is an encouragement to put aside, and discard as a dirty garment, all sinful practices. This must extend to sins of thought and feeling, as well as speech and actions; to everything corrupt and sinful. We must yield ourselves to the word of God, with humble and teachable minds. Being willing to hear about our faults, taking it not only patiently, but thankfully. The purpose of the word of God is to make us wise for salvation; and those who seek unimportant or low goals when studying it, dishonor the message and disappoint themselves.

If we heard a sermon every day of the week, and an angel from heaven were the preacher, yet, if we only rested in hearing, it would never bring us to heaven. Mere listeners deceive themselves; and self-deception will be found to be the worst deception at last. If we flatter ourselves, it is our own fault; the truth, as it is in Jesus, flatters no one. Let the word of truth be carefully attended to, and it will show us the corruption of our nature, the disorder of our hearts and lives; and it will tell us plainly what we are. Our sins are the spots that the law reveals: Christ’s blood is the cleansing water that the message shows. But in vain do we hear God's word, and look into the gospel mirror, if we go away, and forget our spots instead of washing them off; and forget our remedy, instead of applying it. This is the case with those who do not hear the word as they should. In hearing the word, we seek guidance and direction from it, and when we study it, it becomes a source of spiritual life. Those who follow God’s law and word will be blessed in all their ways. His gracious reward later will be connected to his present peace and comfort. Every part of Divine revelation has its purpose, in bringing the sinner to Christ for salvation, and in directing and encouraging him to walk at liberty, by the Spirit of adoption, according to God’s commands. And remember this clearly: people are blessed not because of their deeds, but despite them. It is not talking, but acting, that will bring us to heaven. Christ will become more precious to the believer's soul, which by his grace will become better prepared for the inheritance of the saints in light.

When people take more pains to appear religious than truly to be so, it is a sign their religion is worthless. Failing to control the tongue, being quick to speak about the faults of others, or to diminish their wisdom and piety, are signs of a worthless religion. The person who has a gossiping tongue cannot have a truly humble, gracious heart. False religion can be recognized by its impurity and lack of kindness. True religion teaches us to do everything as if in God’s presence. An unblemished life must go with sincere love and kindness. Our true religion is equal to the extent that these qualities are present in our hearts and actions. And let us remember, nothing matters in Christ Jesus except faith that works by love, purifies the heart, subdues selfish desires, and obeys God’s commands.
